REASON FOR THIS EXAMINATION:
fx?
HISTORY: Status post trauma, motor vehicle collision.

SUPINE AP VIEW OF THE PELVIS: A cortical defect involving the
inferior aspect of the left acetabulum is identified.
Additionally, just inferior to the left femoral head, there is
an oval, round, calcified structure noted, which may represent a
fracture fragment. The hips are otherwise normally aligned
without evidence of dislocation. No other definite fractures are
noted. A Foley catheter is seen with tip overlying the expected
position of the bladder. Sacroiliac joints appear preserved.

IMPRESSION: Probable acetabular fracture with small fracture
fragment lying inferior to the left femoral head. Additional
oblique or coned down views of the left hip are recommended for
further evaluation.

The study and the report were reviewed by the staff radiologist.
DR. Travis Connor 0526 Bailey Mills
Port Michaelshire, NE 17650
DR. [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] [**Initial (NamePattern1) **] Fuentes
Approved: FRI 2012-01-21 10:24 PM

Reason: LEFT HIP CLOSED REDUCTION

INDICATION: Closed reduction. Left hip dislocation.

FINDINGS: Three intraoperative spot fluoroscopic images were
obtained without the presence of a radiologist. These
demonstrate anatomic alignment of the left femoral head relative
to the acetabulum. Please refer to the operative note for
specific details.
INDICATION: 22-year-old man in a motor vehicle accident.

CT SINUS/MANDIBLE/MAXILLOFACIAL W/O CONTRAST  1985-09-02 9:03 AM

CT SINUS/MANDIBLE/MAXILLOFACIA; CT RECONSTRUCTION

Reason: would like 3D reconstructions with thin cuts

Cornerstone Hospital Of Southwest Louisiana MEDICAL CONDITION:
22 year old man with s/p car vs. tree with mandibular fractures
from outside hospital CT scans
REASON FOR THIS EXAMINATION:
would like 3D reconstructions with thin cuts
CONTRAINDICATIONS for IV CONTRAST: None.

INDICATION: 22-year-old man in a motor vehicle accident.

TECHNIQUE: Mandibular CT with multiplanar reconstructions.

FINDINGS: There is a horizontally oriented fracture of the ramus
of the right mandible. There is no displacement. Also, seen is
an obliquely oriented fracture of the angle of the left mandible
extending toward the posterior most molar tooth, but not
involving the tooth itself. No displacement.

Mild mucosal thickening is visualized in the left maxillary
sinus as well as the sphenoid sinus.

No other fractures or dislocations visualized. The soft tissues
are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: Bilateral undisplaced mandibular fractures.
The study and the report were reviewed by the staff radiologist.
DR. Kyle Summer Ramos
DR. Sarah [**Initials (NamePattern4) **] Haynes
Approved: SAT 1985-09-02 7:40 PM

COMPARISON: 1972-04-17.

INDICATION: Reintubation.

An endotracheal tube is in satisfactory position, terminating 3
cm above the carina. Although a nasogastric tube terminates in
the stomach, the sideport is now several centimeters proximal to
the GE junction level. The heart is upper limits of normal in
size for technique and stable. There are patchy opacities in
both lower lobes, predominantly in the retrocardiac regions,
slightly increased in the interval. These are most likely due to
either atelectasis or aspiration.

IMPRESSION:
1. Endotracheal tube in satisfactory position.
2. Sideport of nasogastric tube is above the GE junction level
and could be advanced by several centimeters for more optimal
placement.

DR. [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] [**Initial (NamePattern1) **] Santana
Approved: MON 1971-05-01 3:45 PM

Reason: STANDING AP PELVIS PLS (AND JUDET VIEW LEFT HIP)

Cornerstone Hospital Of Southwest Louisiana MEDICAL CONDITION:
22 year old man with s/p mva, closed reduction left hip fx
REASON FOR THIS EXAMINATION:
STANDING AP PELVIS PLS (AND JUDET VIEW LEFT HIP)
HISTORY: Post-reduction left hip fracture.

Standing AP pelvis with Judet views. Three views in all.

There is a left femoral head fracture fragment along the
inferomedial aspect of the left femoral head. A separate left
posterior acetabular fracture fragment is also present. No other
fractures are identified. The left hip joint is congruent and
alignment about the pelvis is otherwise within normal limits. No
SI joint or pubic symphysis diastasis is seen. Incidental note
is made of spina bifida occulta at L5.

DR. [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] [**Initial (NamePattern1) **] Daniels


Brief Hospital Course:
Pt arrived in the Memorial Hospital Association ER via transport by 0526 Bailey Mills
Port Michaelshire, NE 17650 from
Central Valley General Hospital.  Pt was immediately evaluated by the emergency
department and trauma surgery teams who ordered the appropriate
studies and lab tests and quickly determined that the patient
had a left hip dislocation, a left acetabular fracture and
bilateral mandibular fractures. The orthopaedics service and
OMFS were immediately consulted, and the pt's hip was relocated
successfully in the OR ASAP by the orthopaedics's service and a
traction pin was placed. All LE pulses were noted to be present
post-procedure. The pt. was then cared for by the trauma surgery
service and 3 doses ancef were administered as prophylaxis for
pt's wounds. The pt proceded to undergo an ORIF of his mandible
fractures by OMFS without incident, was admitted to the TICU
post-op and started on a seven day course of clindamycin, and
soon after the pt. was taken to the OR for an ORIF of the left
acetabulum which occurred without incident.  The pt. was
subsequently started on Lovenox, admitted for a short period of
time to the TICU post-op, then was extubated and transferred to
the floor as he was stabe throughout the entire post-op period.
The pt. underwent rehab and evaluation by PT and OT on the floor
and was subsequently deemed stable, in good condition and well
enough to be discharged home. Pt. to continue to work with PT as
insurance allows and is to follow up with orthopaedics and
oro-maxillo-facial surgery services.

Medications on Admission:
none

Discharge Medications:
1. Oxycodone-Acetaminophen 5-325 mg/5 mL Solution Sig: 5-10 MLs
PO Q4-6H (every 4 to 6 hours) as needed for pain.

twice a day for 10 days: Rinse for at least 30seconds and do not

Disp:*1 500ml* Refills:*0*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
-nondisplaced subcondylar fracture of R mandible and
nondisplaced fracture of body of L mandible
-Left hip dislocation/acetabular fx


Discharge Condition:
good


Discharge Instructions:
Please keep your cast dry and clean.  If you need to take a
shower, please cover it with a waterproof dressing.  Please make
sure to always walk using your walker to assist you. Do not turn
your right leg more than 90 degrees inward, and do not flex your
right leg at your hip to greater than 90 degrees.

-Please take your medications as perscribed

-your discharge mediction includes narcotic pain medication. Do
not drive or operate heavy machinery while taking this
medication as it does hinder your motor ability. Please do not
partake in any activity that requires fine motor skills while
taking this medication as they may compromise your safety.

Followup Instructions:
1) Please call to arrange a follow up appointment in the
Healthsouth Deaconess Rehabilitation Hospital clinic in 2 weeks: 859-455-7408
2) Please call to arrange a follow up appointment in the
Weatherford Regional Hospital clinic in one week and weekly thereafter:
694-627-1678
